evidence: `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` exposes a simple accessor (`getSupabaseClient`) that also performs one-time instrumentation install and runtime initialization, so subsystem boot behavior is hidden behind a generic getter.

`src/domains/generation/types/generationRow.ts` mixes DB-shaped fields (`generation_id`, `timeline_frame`, `based_on`) with UI/runtime fields (`imageUrl`, `isOptimistic`, `hasUnviewedVariants`) in a single high-import shared type.
`src/domains/generation/types/shot.ts` depends on `GenerationRow` and adds more mixed concerns (persisted shape plus computed UI stats), widening cross-feature coupling through shared domain types.

`src/pages/Home/components/CreativePartnerPane.tsx` and `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/components/InfoTooltip.tsx` both wire tooltip interaction policy details directly in feature components, repeating interaction-orchestration responsibilities instead of isolating them behind a narrower component boundary.

Multiple files contain large comment blocks that restate immediate code behavior without adding non-obvious rationale (e.g., src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline/hooks/useTimelinePositions.ts, src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/useClickOutside.ts, src/types/gifenc.d.ts).

Wrapper functions in supabase/functions/_shared/billing.ts are pure pass-throughs to imported helpers (`extractOrchestratorRef`, `buildSubTaskFilter`) while carrying heavy local docs, adding indirection without behavior.

No-op/placeholder defensive branches appear in production paths (supabase/functions/_shared/billing.ts:118-122 and supabase/functions/ai-prompt/index.ts:91-98), indicating generated-style scaffolding kept after implementation.
src/shared/hooks/useDarkMode.ts defines `toggle` as `setDarkMode(!darkMode)`, which captures stale state and can collapse rapid successive toggles into one effective update.

src/shared/hooks/useCopyToClipboard.ts gates copy with `if (!text) return;`, so an empty string is treated as absent input even though it is a valid clipboard payload; src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useCopyToClipboard.test.ts codifies this behavior.
src/shared/lib/autoplayMonitor.ts builds a `changes` array in `logVideoStateChanges` but never emits/returns/uses it, leaving meaningful branch work with no effect.

findings.summary: Singleton accessor hides subsystem bootstrap side effects and boot order assumptions
evidence: `getSupabaseClient()` appears to be a plain getter but triggers instrumentation install and runtime init via `buildSupabaseClient()`.
`handleError()` is another global entrypoint that looks utility-like but performs logging and optional user notification side effects; both patterns blur ownership behind generic names.
findings.suggestion: Split bootstrap from access: add explicit app-start initialization functions (for Supabase runtime/instrumentation and error notifier wiring), keep getters/handlers focused on single responsibilities, and rename side-effectful entrypoints to reflect behavior (for example `initializeSupabaseClientRuntime` + `getSupabaseClient`).

findings.summary: Core generation types combine persistence schema and view-model concerns
evidence: `GenerationRow` includes DB identifiers/foreign keys and UI-oriented flags/counts in one interface used broadly.
`Shot` references `GenerationRow[]` while also carrying computed presentation stats (`imageCount`, `hasUnpositionedImages`), reinforcing a single cross-layer shape.
findings.suggestion: Introduce explicit boundary types: persisted row types (DB-facing), domain entities, and UI view models; then provide mappers at feature boundaries so downstream modules depend on smaller, role-specific contracts.

findings.summary: Tooltip interaction orchestration is duplicated across feature components
evidence: Both feature components directly manage `open/onOpenChange`, mobile behavior, and hover/click handlers via `useTooltipInteractionPolicy`.
The shared tooltip primitives stay low-level, so higher-level interaction policy is repeated in multiple product surfaces.
findings.suggestion: Create a higher-level shared component or hook wrapper (for example `ResponsiveManagedTooltip`) that encapsulates policy wiring and exposes a narrow API, then migrate feature components to that boundary.

findings.summary: Billing helpers add pass-through wrappers with no behavioral value.
evidence: `extractOrchestratorRef` and `buildSubTaskFilter` in billing.ts only forward arguments to imported helpers and return directly.
This file-level wrapper pattern increases API surface and documentation burden while not adding auth/logging/validation behavior.
findings.suggestion: Inline direct imports at call sites (or re-export from an index barrel) and remove the zero-logic wrappers; keep only helpers that enforce local policy.

findings.summary: Several modules contain generated-style explanatory comment volume that exceeds code complexity.
evidence: useTimelinePositions.ts has extensive banner/comment sections and repeated inline rationale around straightforward operations, reducing signal-to-noise while reading logic.
useClickOutside.ts includes long JSDoc examples and parameter docs for a small, conventional hook implementation.
gifenc.d.ts and presetMetadata.ts include verbose top-level/type comments that mostly restate symbol names and obvious field meanings.
findings.suggestion: Trim comments to only non-obvious constraints/decisions, and remove repetitive doc text that mirrors signatures; keep concise API notes where behavior is surprising.

findings.summary: Production code contains no-op branches that look like retained generation scaffolding.
evidence: billing.ts handles `costData.skipped` and numeric `costData.cost` with intentionally empty branches, adding branch complexity without behavior.
ai-prompt/index.ts validates prompt count but keeps a 'too few' branch as comment-only fallback, effectively preserving placeholder control flow.
findings.suggestion: Replace empty branches with explicit normalized handling (e.g., parse response into a typed result object once) or remove branches entirely when behavior is intentionally identical.

findings.summary: `shared/lib` is acting as a catch-all across unrelated ownership boundaries.
evidence: `ProjectScopedCache.ts` is a generic data-structure utility with no browser dependency.
`aspectRatios.ts` mixes domain constants (`ASPECT_RATIO_TO_RESOLUTION`) with UI preview sizing helpers.
`autoplayMonitor.ts` is DOM/dev instrumentation code (video element scanning + interval monitor) but is placed beside generic shared utilities.
findings.suggestion: Split `src/shared/lib` by responsibility: keep generic primitives in `shared/lib/core` (or similar), move media/domain ratio logic to a media/domain package, and move DOM/dev monitoring to a browser diagnostics/debug package.

findings.summary: Infrastructure config files also own product/runtime policy, weakening package boundaries.
evidence: `tailwind.config.ts` imports theme primitives from `./src/styles/tailwind/*`, coupling root tooling config to app source-tree internals.
`vite.config.ts` includes deployment host allowlist values and manual vendor chunk policy in the same root file, combining environment policy with bundler mechanics.
findings.suggestion: Extract app/deployment policy into dedicated config modules (for example under `src/config` or `config/`) and keep root tool configs thin adapters that import those modules.

findings.summary: README runtime contract duplicates canonical Supabase accessor rule, reducing contract clarity.
evidence: README Runtime Contracts includes two bullets stating `getSupabaseClient()` is the canonical lazy accessor.
`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` defines a single canonical accessor (`getSupabaseClient`) and does not require duplicated policy statements.
findings.suggestion: Collapse the duplicated bullets into one canonical statement and keep only additional non-overlapping governance details (for example eager warm-up behavior) in a separate bullet.

findings.summary: Canonical GenerationRow contract mixes persistence and UI concerns in one shared surface.
evidence: README marks `GenerationRow` as a canonical ownership contract.

`GenerationRow` includes DB-column-style fields (`timeline_frame`, `based_on`, `parent_generation_id`, `pair_shot_generation_id`) alongside presentation/domain fields (`imageUrl`, `thumbUrl`, `hasUnviewedVariants`, `derivedCount`) in a single exported interface used across galleries/lightboxes/timeline.
findings.suggestion: Split into at least two explicit contracts (e.g., `PersistedGenerationRow` and `GenerationViewModel`) with a mapper at the domain boundary; keep `GenerationRow` as an alias only temporarily during migration with deprecation notes.

findings.summary: Dark mode toggle uses captured state, making rapid toggles non-deterministic
evidence: `toggle` is implemented as `setDarkMode(!darkMode)` instead of a functional updater.
With batched updates, multiple `toggle()` calls in one tick can read the same captured `darkMode` and apply the same next value.
findings.suggestion: Change toggle to `setDarkMode((prev) => !prev)` and add a test that calls `toggle()` twice in one `act` block to verify it returns to the original state.

findings.summary: Clipboard copy guard conflates empty string with undefined input
evidence: `handleCopy` returns early on `if (!text)`, so `''` is never copied.
The hook type accepts `string | undefined`, but control flow uses truthiness rather than explicit undefined/null checks.
findings.suggestion: Use an explicit nullish check (`if (text == null) return`) so empty string remains copyable; update tests to expect `writeText('')` for empty-string input.
